ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated emping code; A00LC (Crackers and crispbreads) is the closest defensible category match.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical snack serving of emping is approximately 30 g (about 10–15 crackers), providing roughly 108 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Emping — Singapore HPB Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Foodo database entry for Emping (cr_id F12050002). All eight reported nutrients (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, iron) are taken verbatim from the HPB record at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal, American Diabetes Association)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for emping melinjo cracker was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 74 is estimated by analogy to rice crackers / plain puffed-grain crackers (GI 74, glucose reference) reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283), which is a peer-reviewed journal source. Emping is a starch-dominant cracker with low fibre (1.4 g/100 g) and no significant fat or protein to blunt glycaemic response, making this analogy defensible. GL per serving = (74 × 22.2 g available carbohydrate per 30 g serving) / 100 = 16.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (26 mg/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Plain emping contains only 26 mg sodium per 100 g, well below the 200 mg/100 g threshold; however, commercially salted or flavoured variants may carry substantially higher sodium and should be checked on the label before consumption.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High carbohydrate load (73.9 g/100 g) with very low dietary fibre (1.4 g/100 g) and estimated high GI (~74)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Emping has a high estimated glycaemic index (~74) and a glycaemic load of approximately 16 per 30 g serving; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should strictly limit portion size, pair with protein or high-fibre foods to blunt the glycaemic response, and prefer lower-GI snack alternatives.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-frying generates pro-inflammatory advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); fat composition data unavailable
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Although total fat is relatively low at 4.0 g/100 g, emping is typically deep-fried, which generates pro-inflammatory AGEs and may introduce saturated or trans fats depending on the frying medium; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption and choose baked or air-fried preparations where available.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates AGEs; high refined carbohydrate load promotes inflammatory glycaemic response
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Emping is typically deep-fried, producing AGEs that exacerbate joint inflammation in rheumatoid arthritis, and its high refined carbohydrate content (73.9 g/100 g, fibre 1.4 g/100 g) drives an inflammatory glycaemic load; avoidance is recommended for active RA management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Emping (also called emping melinjo or emping belinjo) is a traditional Indonesian cracker made from the seeds of the melinjo tree (Gnetum gnemon), also known as belinjo. The seeds are boiled, peeled, flattened by hand or with a mallet, sun-dried, and then deep-fried until crisp and golden. The result is a thin, irregularly shaped, bite-sized cracker with a distinctive slightly bitter, nutty flavour that sets it apart from other Southeast Asian crackers such as kerupuk. Emping is widely consumed as a snack and is a common accompaniment to Indonesian dishes including soto, gado-gado, and nasi goreng. It is also popular in Singapore and Malaysia as a snack food. Nutritionally, emping is carbohydrate-dominant (73.9 g/100 g) with moderate protein (7.1 g/100 g), low fat (4.0 g/100 g), and very low dietary fibre (1.4 g/100 g). Sodium is very low at 26 mg/100 g in the plain form. Energy density is 360 kcal/100 g. The estimated glycaemic index is high (~74), making emping a high-glycaemic-load snack unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. Deep-frying introduces pro-inflammatory advanced glycation end-products (AGEs), raising concern for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis or cardiovascular disease. Iron content is notable at 4.5 mg/100 g. Emping is naturally gluten-free, vegan, and halal. Commercially prepared variants may contain added salt, significantly increasing sodium content.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, iron) are sourced directly from the HPB Foodo database at confidence 0.95 with an exact match to the named food. GI is an analogous estimate from a peer-reviewed journal source (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care) and is clearly documented as such. Remaining micronutrient fields are set to -1 reflecting genuine data absence in the HPB record.
notes: Fields set to -1 (data not available in HPB Foodo for this entry): saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, sugar_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. These fields are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for Emping (F12050002) as of April 2026; no secondary approved source (USDA FoodData Central, FAO/INFOODS SEA) was found with a sufficiently close match to justify imputation without risking data integrity. GI value of 74 is an analogous estimate based on rice crackers / plain puffed-grain crackers from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283); no direct GI measurement for emping melinjo was located in the University of Sydney GI Database. Chinese name provided is a descriptive translation ('fried melinjo-nut cracker') as no standard Mandarin loanword for emping is in common use; this should be verified by a native-speaker reviewer. Tamil and Hindi local names are not established in common usage for this Indonesian snack and have been left null pending expert review. The input record listed cooking_style as null; emping is traditionally deep-fried but may also be prepared by baking or air-frying; preparation method is documented in classification.preparation_method.
